title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated 1-RTT Key Update Lifecycle Work Item
summary: Plan the trace-safe widening from the current first-successor Key Phase floor to a repeated 1-RTT key-update lifecycle, including old-key retention and discard, without treating the existing first-update helper as full RFC 9001 support.
planned_changes:
- Introduce the repeated-update control state that is still missing above the first-successor floor: current-phase acknowledgment tracking, the three-PTO post-confirmation local-update cooldown for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003`, and the control flags needed to distinguish update-required, update-impossible, and stateless-reset-only behavior.
- Add timing-neutral invalid-Key-Phase classification for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001`, then add the optional `KEY_UPDATE_ERROR` policy for unconfirmed consecutive peer updates under `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003` without creating a distinguishable timing signal.
- Add per-key-set encrypted-packet counters and AEAD-limit-driven update-or-close behavior for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005` through the same repeated-update gate used for ordinary local initiation.
- Preserve the existing first-successor install path as a bounded prerequisite rather than widening its single-update guard in place.
- Keep repeated old/current/next epoch selection and second-successor packet handling blocked until the confirmation/timing/AEAD-policy subset above is proven.
- Keep TLS KeyUpdate prohibition, post-handshake ticket ingestion, 0-RTT, public API promises, and interop expansion out of this RFC 9001 lifecycle slice unless separately traced.
verification_plan: The executable slice must include positive tests for repeated local and peer updates, negative tests for duplicate/stale/tampered transitions, current-phase acknowledgment gating, packet-number-incoherent old-key packets, AEAD-limit-driven update requests, and premature discard, fuzz or property coverage for Key Phase observation and epoch transition boundaries, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for the hot successor-derivation/install/open path. The current first-update tests and QuicApplicationPacketKeyPhaseBenchmarks remain baseline evidence only.
completion_notes: Planned for the broader repeated lifecycle. The direct first-successor proof for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0001 through REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P1-0010 is now executable at the requirement-home level, including retained old first-update 1-RTT packet-protection material before and after new-key packet authentication plus explicit retained cleanup. The first peer-initiated update proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0001, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0002, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0004 for opening next-key packets only when they carry the next Key Phase bit, updating send keys before ACK emission, and allowing KEY_UPDATE_ERROR when a retained old-key ACK packet acknowledges a tracked first-successor phase-1 send. The first next-receive-key retention proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0002 by retaining current and next receive material for apparent peer updates and clearing the retained next material once the first successor is installed. The first send-key ordering proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0001 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0002 for first-update ACK sends and locally built higher-packet-number packets. The first old/current next receive-selection proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P4-0003, REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0001, and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0002 for rejecting old-key packets that violate the first successor packet-number floor, using retained old receive material when recovered packet numbers fall below the current phase floor, and using next receive material when recovered packet numbers rise above that floor. The first retained-old discard proof now covers REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0004 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0005 by arming a three-PTO retained-old discard timer after the first authenticated phase-1 packet and discarding the retained old read material together with matching sender and recovery state when that timer expires. The remaining planned requirement-home creation list is `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003`, `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001`, `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003`, and `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005`; no repeated 1-RTT key-update implementation, receive-key selection beyond the first successor, or repeated old-key discard lifecycle is claimed by this artifact before that control-state subset lands.

supplemental_sections.heading: Current Boundary
supplemental_sections.content: The current runtime can prove the first 0-to-1 successor install, successor write-key use, stable header-protection keys, updated-key protection for subsequent packets, pre-confirmation local-update rejection, bounded repeat-update rejection before any current-phase acknowledgment support exists, successor receive-key installation, retained next first-update receive material, retained old first-update 1-RTT material before and after new-key packet authentication, the first retained-old discard timer after authenticating a new-key packet, first old-key discard with matching sender/recovery cleanup, first peer-initiated next-key packet opening, updated send-key ACK protection for peer-initiated updates, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for retained old-key ACK packets that acknowledge first-successor phase-1 sends, first-update send-key ordering, the first old/current next receive-selection floors for lower and higher recovered packet numbers, the first KEY_UPDATE_ERROR floor for old-key packet-number ordering violations, QUIC `quic ku` successor traffic-secret derivation, and packet key/IV derivation from updated secrets. Repeated key-update cycles after acknowledgment, unconfirmed consecutive peer-update error policy, receive-key selection across retained/current/next epochs beyond the first successor, AEAD-limit update requests, and repeated old 1-RTT key discard remain planned until the lifecycle owner is implemented and proven.

supplemental_sections.heading: Next Planned Scope
supplemental_sections.content:
- Add requirement-home proof for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P5-0003` so repeated local updates stay blocked until the prior update is confirmed and the three-PTO cooldown expires.
- Add requirement-home proof for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P3-0001` and `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P2-0003` so invalid-Key-Phase handling and optional consecutive peer-update errors stay timing-neutral.
- Add requirement-home proof for `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0001` through `REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0005` so AEAD packet counting and update-or-close behavior become explicit before any broader repeated epoch selection is attempted.
- Keep second-successor packet handling and broader repeated epoch selection blocked until the control-state subset above is implemented and proven.
